en_title: Within One Space, Bound by Time
en_body:
Reach out and touch leaves, flowers
Breakfast upon grains
Playfully eat the fruits of trees...
Birds and squirrels frolic so
Morning greets the rising sun
Night greets moon, stars...
Like the ocean
Singing inside
A lovely spiraled shell
Adorning True-Love...
Beloved, do not hide your face
for what:
We share the same
breath
One vault of sky
that is all:
And still are bound
to each other
By time tying tight
Love's bow of ribbon
Call it longing
that we may be near
Longing
is
our faithful shared Memory
between us!
Call it far, yes then it is far
So why does one
keep thinking of the other
for what?
note: The poem turns shared space and time into a ribbon binding separated lovers, with “thương nhớ/nhớ thương” reversing the same words for love and longing.
